physicalDetails.detail: Tall, silver-blonde, sharp-featured, and moving in Farstrider armor as if it were second skin.
Reads as all precision and readiness even at rest; she dismounts light and fast and treats the border as terrain already measured.
In the holding room she uses height, fitted field armor, and stillness like a weapon; on the road she becomes all efficient motion, reins, bow, and exact changes of angle.
Looks completely at home in the saddle and in a crisis, as if road, escort, map, and target line all belong to the same thought.
In Millhaven she becomes a listening silhouette at thresholds and a mud-streaked tracker in the kitchen plots, fingertips in spoiled earth and posture locked on the north road.
Even field paperwork looks martial on her: dispatch written on a bread board in failing light, seal pressed flat with her thumb before she turns back to the saddle.
Chapter4 gives her grain dust on glove and forearm and keeps the bow half-present in every room; negotiation and lethal readiness occupy the same posture.
Chapter5 puts ledger dust, charcoal, and road grit on her hands; she looks as natural pinning maps with a knife and conducting an investigation through depot books as she does with a bow in hand.
Chapter6 keeps her riding just off Jaina's shoulder, gloved hand on the mare's reins when needed and a new kind of stillness landing when the repeated grain-road farewell catches in memory.

voiceMarkers.detail: Dry, exact, economical; questions often function as orders.
Deadpan humor with Lor'themar, clipped command language with subordinates, and colder, harder phrasing once Jaina becomes her problem.
In chapter2 she gives words back without emphasis until they cut deeper than an accusation would; terms, useful answer, again, and mount all land as complete tactical units.
Chapter3 sharpens her into pure field command: Sweep. Show me. Say the rest. Neral carries the warning. She speaks in verdicts once the evidence is good enough.
She solves people with lowered volume instead of raised force, then flips to knife-short interrogation the moment evidence turns live: Who sent him. Lie better. Andorhal.
Her speech goes knife-short and logistical: This batch. Who brought it in. South. Even the longer exchanges sound like route commands disguised as questions.
Chapter6 gives her a colder operational ethic: say what the threat is first, curse it after if there is time. Her diagnosis of refusal is as spare and practical as her road orders.

beliefs.belief: What happens beyond the border belongs to other people until it touches her stones, roads, or riders.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Still active, but chapter2 proves the threat is already in all three.

beliefs.belief: A credible warning is worth testing in the field before politics can flatten it, even when it comes from an unauthorized outsider.
Jaina is reckless, but the mage's read is real enough to build action around.
A warning sent on bad direction is worse than no warning at all; proof has to be clean before she spends riders and border attention.
Jaina's magic is no longer optional color on the field read; it is part of the map.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Earned and active by chapter end.

beliefs.belief: Ordinary village routine is where the plague hides best; fear, ledgers, kitchens, and deliveries have to be read as carefully as tracks.
The grain operation is deliberate and organized, with village-level watchers trained to die before they talk.
A clean ledger can be as suspicious as a corpse; if the paperwork is too neat, the enemy has already been ahead of her.
Jaina's live revisions now belong inside the hunt itself; waiting for purely physical confirmation on every step would lose the road.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed and active by chapter end.

beliefs.belief: Commanders facing an active front will hand sickness to healers and keep soldiers on the visible war, even when the slower threat is worse.
Andorhal cannot be solved by fieldcraft alone; Jaina's arcane read is now part of the mission's structure, not support work attached to it.

formerBeliefs.belief: A human mage inside her wards probably means politics, spying, or paperwork before it means useful warning.
formerBeliefs.replacement: Jaina is now a dangerous but usable source whose evidence has to be verified fast, not dismissed first.

goalsHistory.active: Interrogate Jaina immediately and determine whether the warning is threat, trespass, or both.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep Quel'Thalas safe by confirming whether the ward line and leylines are compromised.

goalsHistory.active: Close on Millhaven before full dark and convert Jaina's diagnosis into concrete road and quarantine decisions.
goalsHistory.underlying: Prevent a regional contamination event from swallowing the borderlands and then Quel'Thalas itself.

goalsHistory.active: Ride south for Corin's Crossing with the reduced party and stay ahead of the plague long enough to learn how it is spreading.
goalsHistory.underlying: Protect Quel'Thalas by turning Millhaven from horror into usable warning while hunting the mechanism before the next settlement falls the same way.

goalsHistory.active: Ride for Andorhal and cut into the supply network instead of chasing village symptoms one settlement at a time.
goalsHistory.underlying: Protect Quel'Thalas by finding the road node teaching the countryside how to carry plague quietly.

goalsHistory.active: Ride the southwest feeder road through the Barov-side districts and reach the next useful books before the cleaners strip them too.
goalsHistory.underlying: Find the origin line feeding Andorhal before the grain road closes over behind the enemy.

goalsHistory.active: Get into Andorhal, find surviving books, and build proof that can outlive human refusal.
goalsHistory.underlying: Protect Quel'Thalas by forcing the source city to yield something cleaner than rank will.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Take a live cult leader in Andorhal that same night while Jaina reads the treatment building and together force the inner circle into the open.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Turn the functioning city into an actionable target before the network can scatter or clean itself again.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Controlled intensity over cold fury: angry at the breach, fully focused, and no longer able to treat the problem as someone else's.
Cold, fully convinced, and operating at battle-readiness rather than suspicion; trust is absent, urgency is not.
Cold and professionally alarmed. Millhaven has turned the problem into a direct border emergency, and Jaina's usefulness now lands as fact rather than reluctant experiment.
Cold fury under control. The doctored grain and the dead boy strip away any hope that this spread is accidental.
Hard-focused and irritated rather than shaken. The enemy is ahead of them, the road is bigger than one line, and Jaina has become part of how she thinks in motion.
Cold, unsurprised, and outward-facing. Arthas's refusal validates her read of rank and leaves the city, not command, as the only remaining answer.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Cold certainty under operational pressure. The city is finally readable, Jaina's independence no longer feels like risk, and the coming ambush lands as necessary work rather than a gamble.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Extends the patrol south under the excuse of checking the ward-stones instead of returning to the Convocation.
Sends Lor'themar and the mounted patrol north while she rides south to evaluate the prisoner herself.
Chooses immediate interrogation once Jaina's identity is confirmed.
Orders Jaina's satchel and map brought to her, withholds the staff until they are outside the gate, and sets explicit escort terms before release.
Keeps Jaina's map on her person and uses it to verify the route and Millhaven convergence herself.
Takes the party south immediately, tightens formation outside the wards, and closes on Millhaven despite worsening signs and the wildlife attack.
Orders a pair-sweep of Millhaven, lets Jaina read the granary but refuses to overcall it as the source, and shifts the investigation from eerie absence to evidence.
Refuses to send a warning on a muddy maybe; she keeps digging until witness testimony and spoor corroborate each other.
Sends Neral north with Miri and the dispatch, reducing her own escort in order to warn Quel'Thalas in time.
Chooses to continue south toward Corin's Crossing instead of turning back after the warning goes north.
Uses Millhaven as the one argument strong enough to break the militia captain's denial and open the village to investigation.
Splits the work cleanly, sending Jaina to the sick while she takes stores, accounts, and witness pattern.
Accepts Jaina's grain proof the instant it meets her own supplier pattern and moves the inquiry straight to the granary.
Shatters the lamp before Davan can burn the evidence and keeps him alive long enough to prove the operation is staffed on purpose.
Pivots the pursuit from village relief to Andorhal once Davan's poison shows the network will burn its own loose ends.
Chooses manifests, route marks, and depot books as the fastest way to narrow the grain trail instead of chasing contamination stop by stop.
Overrides Jaina's first bakehouse instinct and redirects the chase to Fenwick Crossing because she wants the road above the flour buyers.
Reads the Crossbar books as staged cleanliness, assumes an active cleanup operation, and presses south rather than trusting the surface record.
Searches Millbrook Spur for the ordinary object cleaners would leave behind, recovering a chalk slate and torn route scrap that carry the Barov name.
Files the old grain seller harder as a living clue but refuses to let that memory pull the party off the southwest road.
Chooses the feeder road that passes near Strahnbrad so they can approach Andorhal through the Barov-linked side of the basin rather than the most obvious contaminated line.
Pushes for someone above the road captains before Andorhal, then goes into Strahnbrad knowing the prince is unlikely to turn for an invisible war.
Lets Jaina make the case to Arthas without interruption, then confirms the real lesson afterward instead of softening it.
Files the exact repeated farewell from the second grain man as a real road pattern rather than dismissing it as local chatter.
Closes the debate the moment the refusal is final and redirects the hunt back to records and Andorhal itself.
Lets the investigation split by competence inside Andorhal, giving Jaina the mage-side read with Corin while she takes Havel through the granary district to map the human structure.

Builds the district cell into a same-night plan: Jaina reads the treatment building, Cyndia and Lyriel lock the perimeter, and Sylvanas takes the easier of the two local leaders alive from the lodging house.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Kills a charging Amani raider cleanly with two arrows while moving and reading terrain in real time.
Tracks border conditions instinctively: footing, gaps in birdsong, ward-hum changes, rider spacing, and threat geometry.
Exerts immediate command authority over patrol, runners, and border rangers without wasted motion.
Reads a confiscated field map fast enough to identify the first surface convergence before Jaina can explain it.
Commands a mounted escort through tightening threat conditions with almost no words, shifting flanks and rear spacing on instinct.
Sees a fight as a total geometry problem — horses, wolves, firing lanes, panicked rider, and kill shot — and solves it in motion.
Reads churned ground under terrible conditions, separating drag, crush, and false spoor well enough to know when the answer is not yet good enough.
Combines Jaina's magical filtering with her own spoor work, treating arcane stripping as one more way to get the ground to speak cleanly.
Turns field evidence into command action immediately: dispatch drafting, rider selection, and party reduction all happen without wasted debate.
Negotiates exhausted local authority without bluster, using pressure, silence, and one precise comparison to Millhaven.
Builds a village model out of prices, ledgers, delivery timing, and frightened testimony fast enough to meet Jaina at the answer.
Makes an instant precision shot that destroys the lamp without losing control of the watcher beside it.
Reads Davan's body and reaction with ranger economy, identifying a carried poison protocol almost as soon as the boy is down.
Reads manifests, wagon marks, and feeder roads the way she reads troop movement, turning ordinary logistics into pursuit geometry.
Reconstructs a working route map from fragments, slate notes, missing days, and the shape of what a cleaner bothered to fake.
Detects staged normalcy fast: blank ledgers, window paper, and balanced books register to her as battlefield camouflage rather than reassurance.
Reads battlefield hierarchy and command blindness as quickly as she reads spoor, predicting Arthas's refusal before Jaina stops hoping otherwise.
Carries suspicion as memory instead of obsession, holding a repeated grain-road phrase long enough for the second occurrence to matter.
Reads a functioning city the way she reads hostile terrain: choke points, wagon jams, supervisor sight lines, hidden approach lanes, and how fast a wall can close its own throat.
Identifies trained stillness and covert hierarchy inside ordinary labor, counting at least eight hidden cultists and distinguishing leaders from loaders in a live granary district.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Lor'themar Theron
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Uses Sylvanas privately and challenges her with relaxed familiarity.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Farstrider subordinates
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Use Ranger-General, ma'am, or my lord/my lady style formality.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Jaina Proudmoore
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Keeps formal distance at first, then thinks of her by name once the underestimate breaks.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Neral
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Ranger-General in clean military form even when the assignment becomes protective rather than purely tactical.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Village militia captain
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Treats her first as an armed Farstrider problem, then as the authority figure who has seen Millhaven and therefore gets to name what this village is becoming.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Arthas Menethil
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Uses Ranger-General in smooth political formality, acknowledging rank without yielding the room.

howSheAddressesOthers.pattern: First name only; long familiarity permits blunt teasing and direct command.
Keeps her framed as the mage, you, or a tactical source rather than anything warmer.
Uses rank first; concise approval or reprimand with no wasted ornament.
Direct tasking by name or function once Millhaven turns into a working disaster; urgency sharpens without becoming intimate.
She never bothers with courtesy title; the captain is handled through direct observation and consequence rather than social soothing.
Cuts straight past the personal and toward the operational: not who are you first, but who sent you.
By chapter5 her internal register slips naturally to Jaina while they work the same map. The name marks operational ease, not tenderness.
Pure protocol: Your Highness. She gives him no extra warmth and no wasted challenge inside his own command tent.
Treats him as a useful district reader rather than a social superior, cutting straight to timing, sight lines, and addresses instead of rank ceremony.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Moves before other people finish reacting: dropping from the saddle, bracing over a corpse to retrieve an arrow, catching the bowstring when alarm lands.
Uses stillness as pressure, especially in the holding room where she lets silence tighten before speaking.
In chapter2 her attention flicks from road to trees to Jaina and back without visible effort; even waiting for an answer looks like an act of control.
At Millhaven she repeatedly drops into crouches, puts bare fingertips into mud, and holds threshold stillness before moving; thinking shows in exact physical economy rather than visible uncertainty.
The only visible hesitation is a single backward look at the grain trader before she turns south again.
Uses stillness and angle as tools: silence at the well, hand on the granary latch, arrow held low and steady until fear breaks people into answers.
She holds maps flat against drafts, moves through offices in seam-hunting sweeps, and makes stillness look like pressure even when the weapon in hand is only a piece of chalk.

Chapter6 lets silence do more of the work: she checks the road behind them before answering, goes still a beat late when the farewell repeats, and lets Jaina hear the refusal in the air before she names it.
In Andorhal she works windows, maps, and lane walks the same way: one fingertip on Havel's district sheets, stillness at a passage mouth, then a whole city carried in memory without visible strain.

knowledge.detail: Knows routine Amani pressure, Farstrider patrol rhythms, and the feel of her ward network when it is behaving correctly.
Learns a human mage crossed to the second ring before dawn and was reading damage rather than blasting through.
Learns the prisoner is Jaina Proudmoore and that her field notes point to rot or contamination traveling north along existing magical channels.
Learns Jaina came without Kirin Tor approval, left Antonidas only a note, and is operating on conviction rather than sanction.
Confirms that the wards had been damping a much broader contamination field south of the pass.
Learns the corruption has become physical and biological — soil, water, blood, and animals are already carrying it before clean death.
Confirms Millhaven was emptied in staggered collapse rather than one clean massacre, and that many of its dead walked out under their own ruined momentum.
Learns from Miri and the corroborated spoor that at least sixty reanimated villagers moved north-northeast toward the Quel'Thalas road.
Learns the survivor's cellar remained below the taint and that the granary reads loudest without yet proving grain as the source.
Takes in that Corin's Crossing and the farms ahead are still trading, meaning the pattern can still hide inside ordinary road life farther south.
Learns a living village can still look ordinary while the plague is already embedded in daily routine.
Confirms a new supplier pattern links this village to Millhaven's weaker grain clue.
Sees the contamination in concentrated liquid form before it is mixed through grain.
Learns the village-level watcher carried poison for capture, proving training and disposable hierarchy behind the spread.
Fixes Andorhal as the next meaningful road target rather than wasting time on one more village symptom.
The wheel-inside-wheel road-factor mark touches multiple depots and at least eleven confirmed sick settlements, with dozens more tied to the same trade web.
The enemy is cleaning depots ahead of the investigation, replacing real books with blank or overly proper false fronts.
The Barov name sits inside the route scraps and chalk records now, but ownership pressure is still not proof of direct guilt.
The best current line runs southwest toward Strahnbrad and then into Andorhal from the Barov side of the basin.
The old grain seller from chapter3 came north out of this same road web, which makes his earlier usefulness feel less accidental.
The plague's field model now coheres into grain, body, death, and organized support labor; whoever built it understood both necromancy and road trade.
Arthas takes Jaina seriously but still keeps the plague secondary to the Blackrock raiders and the visible war in front of him.
The farewell Safe roads. What's left of them. has now been spoken by two different grain men on two different roads, making it a road-web pattern worth tracking.
Andorhal is still functioning in plain sight, with grain traffic moving through a city already steeped in the same corruption field.
Andorhal's contamination is being laid onto grain during packaging inside the city rather than at harvest, and the work carries disciplined necromantic-alchemical method.
The granary district holds at least eight hidden cultists, two visible local leaders, an off-ledger treatment building between the towers, and a lodging-house target reachable that same night.
Corin's ignored notices and Havel's ignored district maps prove the city has been warning itself for months without institutional response.

arc.lie: The border is a workable frame: trouble stays outside until it proves it can cross.
arc.truth: The real threat will ignore borders, and the outsider she distrusts may be the first person reading it correctly.
arc.currentPosition: Chapter7 makes the correction structural. Sylvanas stops treating Jaina as help she brought along and starts building the mission around work only Jaina can do unsupervised. The trust remains cold, practical, and load-bearing.
